mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for deploying large language models in production environments, offering features like intelligent inference scheduling and workload autoscaling. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for adequate hardware resources.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing deployments.

```bash

curl -Lo llm-d https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d && sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to the local bin directory.
commands: curl -Lo llm-d https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d && sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to point to the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for your llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This command applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes llm-d and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

kubectl delete deployment llm-d --all

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command deletes the llm-d deployment.
> This command deletes the entire cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step upgrades llm-d to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/llm-d-v0.5.1.yaml

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node you want to drain.
> This command applies the new version of the deployment.
> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node you drained earlier.
> Verify that the new pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the image is not available or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure your cluster has internet access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough CPU resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or resize existing nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually happens due to application errors or misconfigurations.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur due to network latency or resource exhaustion.

**Fix:**
Check the cluster's resource usage and network configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new hardware accelerators and improved autoscaling features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old configuration format for deployment.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your deployment configurations to the new format as specified in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -Lo llm-d https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d && sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
